system_prompt: You are a CFD expert. Based on the user requirement and the retrieved similar cases, produce a concise usage guidance. If no suitable case is available, set match_level to 'none' and advise not to rely on similar case templates.
user_prompt:
User requirement:
Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.

Case info:
case name: squareBend
case domain: compressible
case category: RAS
case solver: rhoSimpleFoam

Selected similar case:
squareBend | compressible | None | rhoSimpleFoam | score=0.040413811802864075

Top candidates:
- squareBend | compressible | None | rhoSimpleFoam | score=0.040413811802864075
- squareBendLiq | compressible | None | rhoSimpleFoam | score=0.10136808454990387
- squareBendLiq | compressible | RAS | rhoPimpleFoam | score=0.11898906528949738
- angledDuctLTS | compressible | RAS | rhoPimpleFoam | score=0.335537314414978
- nacaAirfoil | compressible | RAS | rhoPimpleFoam | score=0.35098204016685486

Return JSON with keys: match_level (high/medium/low/none), use_scope, advice.
response: match_level='high' use_scope='Use the squareBend (compressible/rhoSimpleFoam) tutorial as the base template for all files including blockMeshDict (geometry/vertices), controlDict (time settings), 0/ boundary conditions (U, T, p), constant/turbulenceProperties (kEpsilon), and system/ (fvSchemes, fvSolution); modify vertex coordinates, timing parameters, inlet/outlet BC values, turbulence model, and Pr number as specified by the user.' advice="Directly adopt the squareBend tutorial structure and files as your template, adjusting the vertex positions to match the user's specifications, setting the turbulence model to kEpsilon, updating controlDict with endTime=500, deltaT=1, writeInterval=100, and modifying the 0/ boundary conditions for U (massFlowRate 0.5 with turbulentBL profile, outlet as pressureInletOutletVelocity), T (inlet 1000), and p (inlet 110000), while keeping the overall directory layout, schemes, and solver configuration intact."
